rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in implementation scope when binding to implementation-specific inputs or derived variables, not component type scope.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: component type contracts must use only visible ports or derived outputs, not subcomponent ports.
Implementation-level contracts must reference only variables or ports defined within the implementation context.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use equality declarations (eq) to define boolean conditions from real or integer expressions to avoid type mismatches.
Ensure all comparison literals and arithmetic match the declared type (real vs int) of the referenced ports or variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type AGREE annexes—use only owned or exposed features.
